edges.description: Barrier failure permits rapid capillary leakage.
Fluid loss from the vascular space lowers effective preload and perfusion.
Extravasated fluid accumulates in tissues during the leak phase.
Plasma-volume loss concentrates circulating blood elements.
Protein-rich plasma leakage lowers circulating serum albumin.
Effective circulating-volume loss produces hypotension during acute attacks.
Interstitial fluid accumulation is expressed clinically as generalized edema.

nodes.description: Generalized edema develops as plasma leaks into the interstitial compartment.

evidence.reference_title: The Role of Failing Autonomic Nervous System on Life-Threatening Idiopathic Systemic Capillary Leak Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Capillary leakage leads to hemoconcentration, hypoalbuminemia, generalized edema, and refractory hypovolemic shock with subsequent complications (8).

nodes.description: The central pathogenic event is abrupt failure of the endothelial barrier, allowing proteins and plasma water to escape from the vascular lumen.

evidence.reference_title: The Role of Failing Autonomic Nervous System on Life-Threatening Idiopathic Systemic Capillary Leak Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: In acute phases, endothelial cells sieving properties are almost completely disrupted resulting in massive fluid and protein loss.

nodes.description: Interstitial fluid accumulation follows the capillary leak and contributes to the characteristic edematous phase of the disorder.

evidence.reference_title: The Role of Failing Autonomic Nervous System on Life-Threatening Idiopathic Systemic Capillary Leak Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Capillary leakage leads to hemoconcentration, hypoalbuminemia, generalized edema, and refractory hypovolemic shock with subsequent complications (8).

nodes.description: Acute leak episodes characteristically produce hemoconcentration from rapid plasma-volume loss.

evidence.reference_title: The Role of Failing Autonomic Nervous System on Life-Threatening Idiopathic Systemic Capillary Leak Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Capillary leakage leads to hemoconcentration, hypoalbuminemia, generalized edema, and refractory hypovolemic shock with subsequent complications (8).

nodes.description: Serum albumin falls during episodes because protein-rich plasma escapes the vascular compartment.

evidence.reference_title: The Role of Failing Autonomic Nervous System on Life-Threatening Idiopathic Systemic Capillary Leak Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Capillary leakage leads to hemoconcentration, hypoalbuminemia, generalized edema, and refractory hypovolemic shock with subsequent complications (8).

nodes.description: Acute attacks commonly present with marked hypotension from abrupt intravascular volume depletion.

evidence.reference_title: The Role of Failing Autonomic Nervous System on Life-Threatening Idiopathic Systemic Capillary Leak Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Capillary leakage leads to hemoconcentration, hypoalbuminemia, generalized edema, and refractory hypovolemic shock with subsequent complications (8).

nodes.description: Severe plasma loss produces hypotension and shock physiology during acute attacks.

evidence.reference_title: The Role of Failing Autonomic Nervous System on Life-Threatening Idiopathic Systemic Capillary Leak Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: The resulting shift of fluids and proteins from the intravascular to the interstitial compartment causes the hypovolemic shock that characterizes disease recurrences.

nodes.description: Intravascular proteins and fluid shift into the interstitial compartment, contracting effective circulating volume.

evidence.reference_title: The Role of Failing Autonomic Nervous System on Life-Threatening Idiopathic Systemic Capillary Leak Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: The resulting shift of fluids and proteins from the intravascular to the interstitial compartment causes the hypovolemic shock that characterizes disease recurrences.
